Global Vaccine Availability and Child Mortality

Author

Sujal Boodgere Sunil

Published

April 15, 2026

Introduction to the Crisis

Every year, millions of children lose their lives to entirely preventable diseases. Despite the incredible medical advancements of the 21st century, the gap between vaccine availability and child mortality remains one of the most pressing global health crises of our time. This report, developed to support UNICEF’s mission, explores the stark disparities in healthcare access and highlights the regions where immediate intervention is needed most.

  • The Unseen Shield: Vaccines are a critical, cost-effective line of defense against childhood illness.
  • The Geographic Divide: Child mortality remains disproportionately high in under-resourced regions, pointing to a severe systemic failure in global health equity.

This block loads everything but is hidden from the final report

import polars as pl from plotnine import * import geopandas as gpd

Load the datasets

indicator_1 = pl.read_csv(“unicef_indicator_1.csv”) indicator_2 = pl.read_csv(“unicef_indicator_2.csv”) metadata = pl.read_csv(“unicef_metadata.csv”)

Merge Data

merged_indicators = indicator_1.join(indicator_2, on=[“country”, “time_period”], how=“inner”, suffix=“_mortality”) final_data = merged_indicators.join(metadata, left_on=[“country”, “time_period”], right_on=[“country”, “year”], how=“inner”) clean_data = final_data.rename({“obs_value”: “vaccine_coverage_pct”, “obs_value_mortality”: “child_mortality_rate”}) clean_data = clean_data.drop_nulls(subset=[“vaccine_coverage_pct”, “child_mortality_rate”])
